name: Nuclear Facility Safety Override
description: Attack tree modelling cyber attack against nuclear facility safety instrumented systems aimed at disabling interlocks, spoofing sensor data, and preventing emergency shutdowns
root_objective: Override nuclear facility safety systems to prevent automatic protective responses

nodes.title: Override Nuclear Facility Safety Systems
nodes.description: Compromise SIS/SCS at a nuclear facility to disable interlocks, spoof sensors, or prevent reactor protection system activation

nodes.title: Gain Access to Nuclear OT Network
nodes.description: Penetrate the nuclear facility's Level 2-3 control network (defense-in-depth zones)

nodes.title: Compromise Engineering Workstation via Supply Chain
nodes.description: Trojanise engineering software or updates delivered to the facility's isolated engineering workstations

nodes.title: USB-Based Delivery (Stuxnet-Style)
nodes.description: Introduce malware via removable media carried by an employee or contractor past the air gap

nodes.title: Insider with Physical Access to Protected Area
nodes.description: Requires a trusted insider or compromised contractor with clearance to access the nuclear protected area

nodes.title: Compromise Safety Instrumented System (SIS)
nodes.description: Modify the logic or suppress outputs of the safety PLC/controller that triggers protective actions

nodes.title: Modify Safety PLC Logic (Triton/TRISIS-Style)
nodes.description: Upload modified safety controller logic that disables or delays emergency shutdown functions

nodes.title: Block SIS Communication Bus
nodes.description: Disrupt the communication path between sensors and the SIS controller to prevent trip signals

nodes.title: Spoof Process Sensor Data
nodes.description: Feed false readings to control systems so operators and automatics do not recognise dangerous conditions

nodes.title: Spoof Neutron Flux / Temperature Sensors
nodes.description: Feed false neutron flux or coolant temperature values to the process computer to mask unsafe reactor state

nodes.title: Manipulate Operator Display Values
nodes.description: Alter control room display values without changing actual sensor-to-SIS data path

nodes.title: Prevent Manual Emergency Response
nodes.description: Hinder operator ability to manually initiate SCRAM or other emergency measures

nodes.title: Disable SCRAM Button Interface
nodes.description: Compromise the HMI to suppress or delay manual SCRAM commands from the control room

nodes.title: Overwhelm Alarm System
nodes.description: Generate thousands of false alarms to cause alarm fatigue and distract operators from real safety events
